No calibrated nutrition summary, diet-history, symptom, lab, supplement, or metabolic model is supplied with this fixture.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MCM6/LCT rs4988235 CT call is available for lactase-persistence context. This is not a diagnosis or diet recommendation.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker can provide lactose-related context for discussion, but it does not create a nutritionist plan or explain symptoms by itself.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: No calibrated nutrition summary model, diet-history context, symptom context, lab context, supplement model, metabolic model, and ancestry applicability metadata unavailable.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The report can summarize supplied marker context, but it should not prescribe diet changes, supplements, or metabolic conclusions without validated evidence.

summaryContext: Use this report as a plain-English summary of supplied nutrition-genetics context, not as a nutritionist plan.
Bring symptoms, diet history, labs, medications, and health goals to a clinician or registered dietitian when making nutrition decisions.
Do not change foods, supplements, or treatment based on genotype alone.

references.title: Nutritional Genomics and Direct-to-Consumer Genetic Testing

missingInputs: Authenticated sample-report sections and rows have not been extracted.
No calibrated nutrition summary, diet-history, symptom, lab, supplement, or metabolic model is supplied.
Broader diet, health, lab, medication, and symptom context is not represented in this fixture.
uncertainty: Nutrition decisions depend on genetics plus symptoms, diet history, labs, medical history, medications, environment, and professional review.
A single marker should not be used to create a nutritionist plan, recommend supplements, or infer metabolic disease.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.
The report is educational and does not create a nutritionist plan, prescribe diet changes, recommend supplements, diagnose symptoms, infer metabolic disease, or provide treatment guidance.
Authenticated Sequencing.com sample report rows are still pending.
